papers.title: MOOSE-Star: Unlocking Tractable Training for Scientific Discovery by Breaking the Complexity Barrier
papers.abstract: Abstract MOOSE-Star framework enables efficient training and inference for generative reasoning by addressing combinatorial complexity through decomposed subtasks, hierarchical search, and bounded composition.  					AI-generated summary While large language models (LLMs) show promise in scientific discovery, existing research focuses on inference or feedback-driven training, leaving the direct modeling of the generative reasoning process, P(hypothesis|background) (P(h|b)), unexplored. We demonstrate that directly training P(h|b) is mathematically intractable due to the combinatorial complexity (O(N^k)) inherent in retrieving and composing inspirations from a vast knowledge base. To break this barrier, we introduce MOOSE-Star, a unified framework enabling tractable training and scalable inference. In the best case, MOOSE-Star reduces complexity from exponential to logarithmic (O(log N)) by (1) training on decomposed subtasks derived from the probabilistic equation of discovery, (2) employing motivation-guided hierarchical search to enable logarithmic retrieval and prune irrelevant subspaces, and (3) utilizing bounded composition for robustness against retrieval noise. To facilitate this, we release TOMATO-Star, a dataset of 108,717 decomposed papers (38,400 GPU hours) for training. Furthermore, we show that while brute-force sampling hits a ''complexity wall,'' MOOSE-Star exhibits continuous test-time scaling.

ru.title: Разложение сложности: логарифмический путь к генеративному научному рассуждению
ru.desc: В статье представлена система MOOSE-Star для эффективного обучения и инференса генеративных рассуждений в больших языковых моделях. Авторы показывают, что прямое моделирование процесса научного рассуждения P(гипотеза|фон) математически неразрешимо из-за комбинаторной сложности при поиске и композиции вдохновений из базы знаний. Решение основано на трёх ключевых идеях: обучение на декомпозированных подзадачах, иерархический поиск с мотивационной направленностью и ограниченная композиция для устойчивости к ошибкам поиска. Предложенный метод снижает сложность с экспоненциальной до логарифмической и показывает непрерывную масштабируемость на этапе инференса.

en.title: MOOSE-Star: Simplifying Generative Reasoning with Hierarchical Learning
en.desc: The MOOSE-Star framework addresses the challenges of training and inference in generative reasoning by breaking down complex tasks into manageable subtasks. It tackles the combinatorial complexity of directly modeling the probability of hypotheses given background knowledge, which is typically intractable. By using a hierarchical search approach and bounded composition, MOOSE-Star significantly reduces the computational complexity from exponential to logarithmic. Additionally, the framework is supported by the TOMATO-Star dataset, which provides a large collection of decomposed research papers for effective training.

zh.title: MOOSE-Star：高效生成推理的新框架
zh.desc: MOOSE-Star框架通过分解子任务、层次搜索和有界组合，解决了生成推理中的组合复杂性，从而实现高效的训练和推理。现有研究主要集中在推理或反馈驱动的训练上，而直接建模生成推理过程尚未得到充分探索。我们展示了直接训练P(h|b)在数学上是不可行的，因为从庞大的知识库中检索和组合灵感的组合复杂性是指数级的。MOOSE-Star通过将复杂性从指数级降低到对数级，提供了一种统一的框架，支持可扩展的推理和训练。

papers.title: DARE: Aligning LLM Agents with the R Statistical Ecosystem via Distribution-Aware Retrieval
papers.abstract: Abstract A lightweight retrieval model called DARE incorporates data distribution information into function representations to improve R package retrieval, achieving superior performance over existing embedding models while enabling more reliable statistical analysis through an R-oriented LLM agent.  					AI-generated summary Large Language Model (LLM) agents can automate data-science workflows, but many rigorous statistical methods implemented in R remain underused because LLMs struggle with statistical knowledge and tool retrieval. Existing retrieval-augmented approaches focus on function-level semantics and ignore data distribution, producing suboptimal matches. We propose DARE (Distribution-Aware Retrieval Embedding), a lightweight, plug-and-play retrieval model that incorporates data distribution information into function representations for R package retrieval. Our main contributions are: (i) RPKB, a curated R Package Knowledge Base derived from 8,191 high-quality CRAN packages; (ii) DARE, an embedding model that fuses distributional features with function metadata to improve retrieval relevance; and (iii) RCodingAgent, an R-oriented LLM agent for reliable R code generation and a suite of statistical analysis tasks for systematically evaluating LLM agents in realistic analytical scenarios. Empirically, DARE achieves an NDCG at 10 of 93.47%, outperforming state-of-the-art open-source embedding models by up to 17% on package retrieval while using substantially fewer parameters. Integrating DARE into RCodingAgent yields significant gains on downstream analysis tasks. This work helps narrow the gap between LLM automation and the mature R statistical ecosystem.
affiliations: Department of Applied Mathematics, The Hong Kong Polytechnic University, Hong Kong SAR, China
Department of Data Science and Artificial Intelligence, The Hong Kong Polytechnic University, Hong Kong SAR, China

ru.title: Распределение данных улучшает поиск статистических инструментов для LLM
ru.desc: В работе предложена модель DARE для улучшенного поиска функций в R-пакетах, которая учитывает информацию о распределении данных при создании представлений функций. Авторы создали RPKB — базу знаний R-пакетов из 8191 пакета CRAN и разработали RCodingAgent — LLM-агента для генерации R-кода и статистического анализа. Модель DARE показывает превосходные результаты с NDCG@10 = 93.47%, превосходя существующие модели встраивания на 17% при использовании значительно меньшего числа параметров. Это исследование помогает преодолеть разрыв между автоматизацией на основе LLM и зрелой экосистемой статистических инструментов R.

en.title: DARE: Enhancing R Package Retrieval with Data Distribution Awareness
en.desc: The paper introduces DARE, a lightweight retrieval model designed to enhance the retrieval of R packages by incorporating data distribution information into function representations. This model addresses the limitations of existing embedding models that often overlook the importance of data distribution, leading to less relevant retrieval results. DARE is built on a curated R Package Knowledge Base (RPKB) and integrates distributional features with function metadata, significantly improving retrieval performance. The results show that DARE outperforms current state-of-the-art models while being more efficient, thus bridging the gap between automated data science workflows and the robust statistical methods available in R.

zh.title: DARE：提升R包检索的智能模型
zh.desc: DARE是一种轻量级的检索模型，它将数据分布信息融入函数表示中，以提高R包的检索效果。与现有的嵌入模型相比，DARE在检索性能上表现更优，并且通过R导向的LLM代理实现了更可靠的统计分析。该模型的主要贡献包括构建了一个基于8191个高质量CRAN包的R包知识库，以及融合分布特征和函数元数据的嵌入模型。通过实验证明，DARE在包检索任务中取得了93.47%的NDCG，超越了现有的开源嵌入模型。

papers.title: SageBwd: A Trainable Low-bit Attention
papers.abstract: Abstract Research investigates why low-bit attention methods like SageBwd exhibit performance gaps during pre-training and identifies key factors for stable training with reduced precision.  					AI-generated summary Low-bit attention, such as SageAttention, has emerged as an effective approach for accelerating model inference, but its applicability to training remains poorly understood. In prior work, we introduced SageBwd, a trainable INT8 attention that quantizes six of seven attention matrix multiplications while preserving fine-tuning performance. However, SageBwd exhibited a persistent performance gap to full-precision attention (FPA) during pre-training. In this work, we investigate why this gap occurs and demonstrate that SageBwd matches full-precision attention during pretraining. Through experiments and theoretical analysis, we reach a few important insights and conclusions: (i) QK-norm is necessary for stable training at large tokens per step, (ii) quantization errors primarily arise from the backward-pass score gradient dS, (iii) reducing tokens per step enables SageBwd to match FPA performance in pre-training, and (iv) K-smoothing remains essential for training stability, while Q-smoothing provides limited benefit during pre-training.

ru.title: Достижение стабильности низкоразрядного внимания при предварительном обучении трансформеров
ru.desc: Статья исследует низкоразрядное внимание (low-bit attention) в трансформер-моделях, в частности метод SageBwd, который квантизирует большинство матричных умножений в механизме внимания до INT8. Авторы выявили ключевые факторы нестабильности при предварительном обучении и предложили решения: нормализация QK критична при больших значениях tokens per step, основные ошибки квантизации возникают в градиентах оценок обратного прохода. Исследование показывает, что SageBwd может достичь производительности полнопроцизионного внимания при правильной настройке гиперпараметров и применении K-smoothing. Эти результаты важны для практического ускорения как предварительного обучения, так и инференса нейросетевых моделей.

en.title: Bridging the Gap: Enhancing Low-Bit Attention for Stable Training
en.desc: This paper explores the challenges of using low-bit attention methods, specifically SageBwd, during the pre-training phase of machine learning models. It identifies that while SageBwd can effectively quantize attention computations, it initially shows a performance gap compared to full-precision attention methods. The authors conduct experiments to understand the reasons behind this gap and find that factors like QK-norm and token reduction are crucial for achieving stable training. Their findings suggest that addressing quantization errors and implementing K-smoothing can help SageBwd perform on par with full-precision attention during pre-training.

zh.title: 低位注意力的稳定训练与性能提升
zh.desc: 本研究探讨了低位注意力方法（如SageBwd）在预训练过程中表现差距的原因，并识别了在降低精度下实现稳定训练的关键因素。SageBwd是一种可训练的INT8注意力机制，能够在保持微调性能的同时量化六个注意力矩阵乘法。研究发现，SageBwd在预训练期间与全精度注意力（FPA）相比存在持续的性能差距，但通过实验和理论分析，证明了SageBwd可以在预训练中与FPA匹配。重要的结论包括：QK-norm对于大步长的稳定训练是必要的，量化误差主要来自反向传播的得分梯度，减少每步的token数量可以使SageBwd在预训练中达到FPA的性能，以及K平滑对训练稳定性至关重要，而Q平滑在预训练中提供的好处有限。

papers.title: HiFi-Inpaint: Towards High-Fidelity Reference-Based Inpainting for Generating Detail-Preserving Human-Product Images
papers.abstract: Abstract HiFi-Inpaint generates high-fidelity human-product images using shared enhancement attention and detail-aware loss with a new 40K-image dataset.  					AI-generated summary Human-product images, which showcase the integration of humans and products, play a vital role in advertising, e-commerce, and digital marketing. The essential challenge of generating such images lies in ensuring the high-fidelity preservation of product details. Among existing paradigms, reference-based inpainting offers a targeted solution by leveraging product reference images to guide the inpainting process. However, limitations remain in three key aspects: the lack of diverse large-scale training data, the struggle of current models to focus on product detail preservation, and the inability of coarse supervision for achieving precise guidance. To address these issues, we propose HiFi-Inpaint, a novel high-fidelity reference-based inpainting framework tailored for generating human-product images. HiFi-Inpaint introduces Shared Enhancement Attention (SEA) to refine fine-grained product features and Detail-Aware Loss (DAL) to enforce precise pixel-level supervision using high-frequency maps. Additionally, we construct a new dataset, HP-Image-40K, with samples curated from self-synthesis data and processed with automatic filtering. Experimental results show that HiFi-Inpaint achieves state-of-the-art performance, delivering detail-preserving human-product images.
affiliations: The Chinese University of Hong Kong
University of Chinese Academy of Sciences

ru.title: Сохранение деталей продукта в нейросетевых композициях человека с товаром
ru.desc: В работе предлагается HiFi-Inpaint — фреймворк для генерации высококачественных изображений человека с продуктом на основе reference-based inpainting. Ключевая инновация заключается в использовании Shared Enhancement Attention для сохранения мелких деталей продукта и Detail-Aware Loss, которая применяет пиксельный уровень контроля через high-frequency карты. Авторы создали новый датасет HP-Image-40K с 40 тысячами тщательно отобранных изображений, полученных из синтетических данных и обработанных автоматической фильтрацией. Экспериментальные результаты показывают, что предложенный метод достигает state-of-the-art производительности и эффективно сохраняет детали продуктов при создании реалистичных композиций.

en.title: HiFi-Inpaint: Elevating Human-Product Image Generation
en.desc: HiFi-Inpaint is a novel framework designed to generate high-quality images of humans interacting with products, which is crucial for advertising and e-commerce. It addresses the challenges of preserving product details by using a technique called Shared Enhancement Attention (SEA) to focus on fine-grained features. Additionally, it employs Detail-Aware Loss (DAL) to ensure precise pixel-level accuracy in the generated images. The framework is trained on a new dataset, HP-Image-40K, which enhances its ability to produce state-of-the-art human-product images.

zh.title: 高保真图像修复，提升人产品结合效果
zh.desc: HiFi-Inpaint是一种新颖的高保真参考图像修复框架，专门用于生成高质量的人物与产品图像。该方法通过共享增强注意力（SEA）来细化产品特征，并使用细节感知损失（DAL）进行精确的像素级监督。为了解决现有模型在多样性训练数据和产品细节保留方面的不足，HiFi-Inpaint构建了一个新的40K图像数据集HP-Image-40K。实验结果表明，HiFi-Inpaint在生成保留细节的人物与产品图像方面达到了最先进的性能。

papers.title: DreamWorld: Unified World Modeling in Video Generation
papers.abstract: Abstract DreamWorld introduces a unified framework for video generation that integrates multiple types of world knowledge through joint modeling of temporal dynamics, spatial geometry, and semantic consistency, addressing limitations in existing models' understanding of physical and temporal relationships.  					AI-generated summary Despite impressive progress in video generation, existing models remain limited to surface-level plausibility, lacking a coherent and unified understanding of the world. Prior approaches typically incorporate only a single form of world-related knowledge or rely on rigid alignment strategies to introduce additional knowledge. However, aligning the single world knowledge is insufficient to constitute a world model that requires jointly modeling multiple heterogeneous dimensions (e.g., physical commonsense, 3D and temporal consistency). To address this limitation, we introduce DreamWorld, a unified framework that integrates complementary world knowledge into video generators via a Joint World Modeling Paradigm, jointly predicting video pixels and features from foundation models to capture temporal dynamics, spatial geometry, and semantic consistency. However, naively optimizing these heterogeneous objectives can lead to visual instability and temporal flickering. To mitigate this issue, we propose Consistent Constraint Annealing (CCA) to progressively regulate world-level constraints during training, and Multi-Source Inner-Guidance to enforce learned world priors at inference. Extensive evaluations show that DreamWorld improves world consistency, outperforming Wan2.1 by 2.26 points on VBench. Code will be made publicly available at https://github.com/ABU121111/DreamWorld{mypink{Github}}.
affiliations: Shanghai Jiao Tong University, Shanghai, China
University of Science and Technology of China, Hefei, China

ru.title: Мир, понятный моделью: совместное моделирование физики, геометрии и семантики в генерации видео
ru.desc: DreamWorld представляет единую архитектуру для генерации видео, которая совместно моделирует временную динамику, пространственную геометрию и семантическую согласованность. Авторы предлагают парадигму Joint World Modeling для интеграции множественных типов знаний о мире в видеогенератор. Для решения проблемы нестабильности при оптимизации разнородных целей предложены методы Consistent Constraint Annealing и Multi-Source Inner-Guidance. Экспериментальные результаты показывают значительное улучшение согласованности в генерируемых видеоконтентах.

en.title: DreamWorld: Unifying Knowledge for Better Video Generation
en.desc: DreamWorld is a new framework for generating videos that combines different types of knowledge about the world. It addresses the shortcomings of previous models that only understood surface-level details and lacked a deep understanding of physical and temporal relationships. By using a Joint World Modeling Paradigm, DreamWorld predicts video pixels and features while considering temporal dynamics, spatial geometry, and semantic consistency. To ensure stability in the generated videos, it introduces techniques like Consistent Constraint Annealing and Multi-Source Inner-Guidance during training and inference.

zh.title: 梦境世界：视频生成的新框架
zh.desc: DreamWorld 是一个统一的视频生成框架，能够通过联合建模时间动态、空间几何和语义一致性来整合多种世界知识。这一方法解决了现有模型在理解物理和时间关系方面的局限性。DreamWorld 采用联合世界建模范式，能够同时预测视频像素和特征，从而捕捉视频的时间动态和空间结构。通过引入一致性约束退火（CCA）和多源内部引导，DreamWorld 在训练和推理过程中有效地提高了世界一致性。

papers.title: AgentVista: Evaluating Multimodal Agents in Ultra-Challenging Realistic Visual Scenarios
papers.abstract: Abstract AgentVista presents a comprehensive benchmark for multimodal agents requiring long-horizon tool interactions across multiple modalities and complex real-world scenarios.  					AI-generated summary Real-world multimodal agents solve multi-step workflows grounded in visual evidence. For example, an agent can troubleshoot a device by linking a wiring photo to a schematic and validating the fix with online documentation, or plan a trip by interpreting a transit map and checking schedules under routing constraints. However, existing multimodal benchmarks mainly evaluate single-turn visual reasoning or specific tool skills, and they do not fully capture the realism, visual subtlety, and long-horizon tool use that practical agents require. We introduce AgentVista, a benchmark for generalist multimodal agents that spans 25 sub-domains across 7 categories, pairing realistic and detail-rich visual scenarios with natural hybrid tool use. Tasks require long-horizon tool interactions across modalities, including web search, image search, page navigation, and code-based operations for both image processing and general programming. Comprehensive evaluation of state-of-the-art models exposes significant gaps in their ability to carry out long-horizon multimodal tool use. Even the best model in our evaluation, Gemini-3-Pro with tools, achieves only 27.3% overall accuracy, and hard instances can require more than 25 tool-calling turns. We expect AgentVista to accelerate the development of more capable and reliable multimodal agents for realistic and ultra-challenging problem solving.
affiliations: 1. Hong Kong University of Science and Technology, Hong Kong
2. University of Science and Technology of China, Hefei, China

ru.title: Оценка реальных способностей мультимодальных агентов в долгосрочном взаимодействии с инструментами
ru.desc: AgentVista представляет комплексный бенчмарк для оценки мультимодальных агентов, которые решают многошаговые задачи с использованием инструментов и визуальной информации в реальных сценариях. Бенчмарк включает 25 подобластей, охватывающих 7 категорий, и требует от агентов выполнять длительные последовательности операций с различными модальностями: поиск в интернете, анализ изображений, навигацию по страницам и программирование. Оценка современных моделей показала существенные пробелы в способности выполнять сложные мультимодальные задачи, при этом даже лучшая модель достигла лишь 27,3% точности. Авторы ожидают, что AgentVista ускорит развитие более продвинутых и надёжных мультимодальных агентов для решения практических и сверхсложных задач.

en.title: Empowering Multimodal Agents for Real-World Challenges
en.desc: AgentVista is a new benchmark designed for multimodal agents that need to perform complex tasks over extended periods. It focuses on real-world scenarios where agents must use visual information and various tools to complete multi-step workflows. Unlike previous benchmarks that only assess simple visual reasoning, AgentVista evaluates agents on their ability to handle intricate tasks that require long-horizon interactions across different modalities. The findings reveal that current state-of-the-art models struggle with these challenges, highlighting the need for improved multimodal capabilities in AI systems.

zh.title: AgentVista：推动多模态智能体的长时间工具交互能力
zh.desc: AgentVista是一个全面的基准测试，旨在评估多模态智能体在复杂现实场景中进行长时间工具交互的能力。现有的多模态基准主要关注单次视觉推理或特定工具技能，未能充分反映实际智能体所需的真实感和视觉细微差别。AgentVista涵盖25个子领域，结合真实且细节丰富的视觉场景与自然的混合工具使用，任务需要跨模态进行长时间的工具交互。我们的评估显示，当前最先进的模型在执行长时间多模态工具使用时存在显著差距，期待AgentVista能加速更强大和可靠的多模态智能体的发展。

papers.title: RealWonder: Real-Time Physical Action-Conditioned Video Generation
papers.abstract: Abstract RealWonder enables real-time action-conditioned video generation by integrating 3D reconstruction, physics simulation, and a distilled video generator to simulate physical consequences of 3D actions.  					AI-generated summary Current video generation models cannot simulate physical consequences of 3D actions like forces and robotic manipulations, as they lack structural understanding of how actions affect 3D scenes. We present RealWonder, the first real-time system for action-conditioned video generation from a single image. Our key insight is using physics simulation as an intermediate bridge: instead of directly encoding continuous actions, we translate them through physics simulation into visual representations (optical flow and RGB) that video models can process. RealWonder integrates three components: 3D reconstruction from single images, physics simulation, and a distilled video generator requiring only 4 diffusion steps. Our system achieves 13.2 FPS at 480x832 resolution, enabling interactive exploration of forces, robot actions, and camera controls on rigid objects, deformable bodies, fluids, and granular materials. We envision RealWonder opens new opportunities to apply video models in immersive experiences, AR/VR, and robot learning. Our code and model weights are publicly available in our project website: https://liuwei283.github.io/RealWonder/

ru.title: Физика как мост между действием и видео в реальном времени
ru.desc: RealWonder — это система для генерации видео в реальном времени, управляемого действиями пользователя. Система объединяет трёхмерную реконструкцию сцены, физическую симуляцию и дистиллированный генератор видео для воспроизведения физических последствий трёхмерных действий. Ключевая идея заключается в использовании физической симуляции как промежуточного звена: действия переводятся в оптический поток и RGB-представления, которые могут обработать модели видеогенерации. Система достигает скорости 13,2 кадров в секунду и позволяет интерактивно исследовать воздействие сил, манипуляций роботов и управление камерой на различные объекты и материалы.

en.title: Real-Time Video Generation with Physics-Driven Actions
en.desc: RealWonder is a novel system that generates videos in real-time based on actions taken in a 3D environment. It combines 3D reconstruction, physics simulation, and a distilled video generator to accurately depict how actions affect physical scenes. By using physics simulation as a bridge, it translates actions into visual representations that can be processed by video models. This approach allows for interactive exploration of various physical phenomena, making it useful for applications in augmented reality, virtual reality, and robotics.

zh.title: 实时视频生成的新突破
zh.desc: RealWonder 是一个实时的动作条件视频生成系统，能够通过整合 3D 重建、物理模拟和精简的视频生成器来模拟 3D 动作的物理后果。该系统的关键在于使用物理模拟作为中介，将连续动作转化为视频模型可以处理的视觉表示。RealWonder 仅需从单张图像中重建 3D 结构，并通过物理模拟生成光流和 RGB 图像，最终实现视频生成。该系统在 480x832 分辨率下可达到每秒 13.2 帧，支持对刚体、可变形物体、流体和颗粒材料的交互式探索。

papers.title: Towards Multimodal Lifelong Understanding: A Dataset and Agentic Baseline
papers.abstract: Abstract MM-Lifelong dataset captures natural video sequences across multiple temporal scales to evaluate multimodal lifelong understanding, revealing limitations in current approaches and introducing a recursive agent framework for improved performance.  					AI-generated summary While datasets for video understanding have scaled to hour-long durations, they typically consist of densely concatenated clips that differ from natural, unscripted daily life. To bridge this gap, we introduce MM-Lifelong, a dataset designed for Multimodal Lifelong Understanding. Comprising 181.1 hours of footage, it is structured across Day, Week, and Month scales to capture varying temporal densities. Extensive evaluations reveal two critical failure modes in current paradigms: end-to-end MLLMs suffer from a Working Memory Bottleneck due to context saturation, while representative agentic baselines experience Global Localization Collapse when navigating sparse, month-long timelines. To address this, we propose the Recursive Multimodal Agent (ReMA), which employs dynamic memory management to iteratively update a recursive belief state, significantly outperforming existing methods. Finally, we establish dataset splits designed to isolate temporal and domain biases, providing a rigorous foundation for future research in supervised learning and out-of-distribution generalization.

ru.title: Рекурсивный агент для понимания видео в масштабе жизни
ru.desc: Авторы представляют новый датасет MM-Lifelong для оценки способности мультимодальных моделей понимать длинные видеопоследовательности в естественных условиях, содержащий 181.1 часа видео с различными временными масштабами. В работе выявлены два ключевых недостатка современных подходов: переполнение рабочей памяти в end-to-end мультимодальных языковых моделях и глобальная потеря локализации в агентных системах при работе с разреженными месячными временными шкалами. Для решения этих проблем предложена архитектура Recursive Multimodal Agent (ReMA), которая использует динамическое управление памятью и рекурсивное обновление состояния убеждения. Работа включает специально разработанные разбиения датасета для изоляции временных и доменных смещений, обеспечивая надежную основу для будущих исследований в обучении с учителем и обобщении на распределение вне выборки.

en.title: Enhancing Video Understanding with Recursive Multimodal Agents
en.desc: The MM-Lifelong dataset is designed to enhance video understanding by capturing natural sequences over different time scales, such as days, weeks, and months. It highlights the limitations of current machine learning models, particularly the Working Memory Bottleneck and Global Localization Collapse, which hinder performance in real-world scenarios. To overcome these challenges, the paper introduces the Recursive Multimodal Agent (ReMA), which utilizes dynamic memory management to improve context handling and belief state updates. This framework not only outperforms existing methods but also sets a new standard for evaluating temporal and domain biases in future research.

zh.title: 多模态终身理解的新突破
zh.desc: MM-Lifelong数据集捕捉了自然视频序列，涵盖多个时间尺度，以评估多模态的终身理解能力。该数据集包含181.1小时的录像，按天、周和月的尺度结构化，以反映不同的时间密度。研究发现，当前方法存在两个主要问题：端到端的多模态终身学习模型在上下文饱和时会遭遇工作记忆瓶颈，而代表性智能基线在处理稀疏的月度时间线时会出现全局定位崩溃。为了解决这些问题，我们提出了递归多模态智能体（ReMA），通过动态记忆管理来迭代更新递归信念状态，显著提高了性能。

papers.title: KARL: Knowledge Agents via Reinforcement Learning
papers.abstract: Abstract A reinforcement learning system for enterprise search agents achieves superior performance through diverse training data generation and multi-task learning approaches.  					AI-generated summary We present a system for training enterprise search agents via reinforcement learning that achieves state-of-the-art performance across a diverse suite of hard-to-verify agentic search tasks. Our work makes four core contributions. First, we introduce KARLBench, a multi-capability evaluation suite spanning six distinct search regimes, including constraint-driven entity search, cross-document report synthesis, tabular numerical reasoning, exhaustive entity retrieval, procedural reasoning over technical documentation, and fact aggregation over internal enterprise notes. Second, we show that models trained across heterogeneous search behaviors generalize substantially better than those optimized for any single benchmark. Third, we develop an agentic synthesis pipeline that employs long-horizon reasoning and tool use to generate diverse, grounded, and high-quality training data, with iterative bootstrapping from increasingly capable models. Fourth, we propose a new post-training paradigm based on iterative large-batch off-policy RL that is sample efficient, robust to train-inference engine discrepancies, and naturally extends to multi-task training with out-of-distribution generalization. Compared to Claude 4.6 and GPT 5.2, KARL is Pareto-optimal on KARLBench across cost-quality and latency-quality trade-offs, including tasks that were out-of-distribution during training. With sufficient test-time compute, it surpasses the strongest closed models. These results show that tailored synthetic data in combination with multi-task reinforcement learning enables cost-efficient and high-performing knowledge agents for grounded reasoning.

ru.title: Многозадачное обучение с подкреплением для интеллектуальных корпоративных поисковых агентов
ru.desc: В статье представлена система обучения агентов для поиска в корпоративных данных с использованием обучения с подкреплением, которая достигает передовых результатов благодаря разнообразным данным обучения. Авторы разработали KARLBench — комплексный набор эталонов, охватывающий шесть различных типов поисковых задач, от поиска сущностей с ограничениями до синтеза отчётов и рассуждений над таблицами. Ключевой вклад заключается в создании конвейера синтеза данных, который использует долгосрочное рассуждение и инструменты для порождения качественных обучающих примеров, с итеративным улучшением моделей. Предложенный метод многозадачного обучения с подкреплением в режиме оффлайн показал, что модель KARL превосходит закрытые модели и обеспечивает оптимальный баланс между качеством, стоимостью и задержкой.

en.title: Empowering Enterprise Search with Reinforcement Learning
en.desc: This paper presents a reinforcement learning system designed for enterprise search agents, which significantly enhances their performance through diverse training data and multi-task learning. The authors introduce KARLBench, a comprehensive evaluation suite that tests various search tasks, demonstrating that models trained on diverse behaviors outperform those focused on single tasks. They also develop a synthesis pipeline that generates high-quality training data using long-horizon reasoning and tool use, improving the training process. Finally, the paper proposes a new post-training method that is efficient and robust, allowing for better generalization across different tasks and scenarios.

zh.title: 多任务强化学习提升企业搜索代理性能
zh.desc: 本文提出了一种基于强化学习的企业搜索代理训练系统，通过多样化的训练数据生成和多任务学习方法，取得了优异的性能。我们引入了KARLBench，这是一个涵盖六种不同搜索模式的多能力评估套件，能够有效评估代理的搜索能力。研究表明，跨异构搜索行为训练的模型在泛化能力上显著优于针对单一基准优化的模型。此外，我们开发了一种代理合成管道，利用长时间推理和工具使用生成多样化、高质量的训练数据，提升了知识代理的性能和效率。

papers.title: Locality-Attending Vision Transformer
papers.abstract: Abstract Vision transformers are enhanced for segmentation tasks through a Gaussian kernel modulation that improves local attention while maintaining classification performance.  					AI-generated summary Vision transformers have demonstrated remarkable success in classification by leveraging global self-attention to capture long-range dependencies. However, this same mechanism can obscure fine-grained spatial details crucial for tasks such as segmentation. In this work, we seek to enhance segmentation performance of vision transformers after standard image-level classification training. More specifically, we present a simple yet effective add-on that improves performance on segmentation tasks while retaining vision transformers' image-level recognition capabilities. In our approach, we modulate the self-attention with a learnable Gaussian kernel that biases the attention toward neighboring patches. We further refine the patch representations to learn better embeddings at patch positions. These modifications encourage tokens to focus on local surroundings and ensure meaningful representations at spatial positions, while still preserving the model's ability to incorporate global information. Experiments demonstrate the effectiveness of our modifications, evidenced by substantial segmentation gains on three benchmarks (e.g., over 6% and 4% on ADE20K for ViT Tiny and Base), without changing the training regime or sacrificing classification performance. The code is available at https://github.com/sinahmr/LocAtViT/.

ru.title: Локальное внимание в трансформерах для точной сегментации изображений
ru.desc: Исследование предлагает способ улучшить Vision Transformers для задач семантической сегментации, сохраняя при этом высокую точность классификации. Основная идея заключается в модуляции механизма self-attention с помощью обучаемого гауссова ядра, которое смещает внимание модели в сторону соседних патчей изображения. Авторы также уточняют представления патчей, чтобы научить модель лучше выделять признаки в пространственных позициях. Эксперименты показывают значительное улучшение производительности на задачах сегментации (более 6% на бенчмарке ADE20K) без изменения процедуры обучения и без потери возможностей классификации.

en.title: Enhancing Segmentation in Vision Transformers with Local Attention
en.desc: This paper presents an enhancement to vision transformers for better segmentation tasks by using a Gaussian kernel modulation. The authors aim to improve local attention, which is important for capturing fine details in images, while still maintaining the model's strong classification abilities. By adjusting the self-attention mechanism to focus more on neighboring patches, the model learns better representations of spatial information. The results show significant improvements in segmentation performance on multiple benchmarks without compromising the original classification capabilities of the vision transformers.

zh.title: 提升视觉变换器的分割性能
zh.desc: 本文提出了一种改进视觉变换器（Vision Transformers）在分割任务中的表现的方法。通过引入可学习的高斯核调制，增强了局部注意力，同时保持了分类性能。该方法使得模型在处理图像时，能够更好地关注邻近的图像块，从而提高了分割的精度。实验结果表明，在多个基准测试中，改进后的模型在分割任务上取得了显著的提升。

papers.title: On-Policy Self-Distillation for Reasoning Compression
papers.abstract: Abstract OPSDC enables efficient reasoning model compression by having models distill concise behavior from their own outputs, achieving significant token reduction while maintaining accuracy.  					AI-generated summary Reasoning models think out loud, but much of what they say is noise. We introduce OPSDC (On-Policy Self-Distillation for Reasoning Compression), a method that teaches models to reason more concisely by   distilling their own concise behavior back into themselves. The entire approach reduces to one idea: condition the same model on a "be concise" instruction to obtain teacher logits, and minimize per-token   reverse KL on the student's own rollouts. No ground-truth answers, no token budgets, no difficulty estimators. Just self-distillation. Yet this simplicity belies surprising sophistication: OPSDC automatically   compresses easy problems aggressively while preserving the deliberation needed for hard ones. On Qwen3-8B and Qwen3-14B, we achieve 57-59% token reduction on MATH-500 while improving accuracy by 9-16 points   absolute. On AIME 2024, the 14B model gains 10 points with 41% compression. The secret? Much of what reasoning models produce is not just redundant-it is actively harmful, compounding errors with every   unnecessary token.

ru.title: Самодистилляция рассуждений: меньше токенов, лучше качество
ru.desc: Статья представляет метод OPSDC для сжатия моделей рассуждения путём самодистилляции. Основная идея заключается в том, чтобы модель обучалась генерировать более лаконичные рассуждения, используя свои собственные выходы в качестве учителя с добавленной инструкцией «будь кратче». Метод минимизирует расхождение Кульбака-Лейблера между выходами студента и учителя без использования эталонных ответов или бюджетов токенов. Результаты показывают значительное сокращение количества токенов (41-59%) при одновременном улучшении точности на задачах MATH-500 и AIME 2024.

en.title: Efficient Reasoning Through Self-Distillation
en.desc: The paper introduces OPSDC, a method for improving reasoning models by teaching them to produce more concise outputs through self-distillation. By conditioning the model on a 'be concise' instruction, it learns to minimize unnecessary information while maintaining accuracy. This approach allows for significant token reduction, achieving up to 59% less output while improving performance on tasks. OPSDC demonstrates that eliminating redundant and harmful tokens can enhance the reasoning process, especially in complex problems.

zh.title: 自蒸馏：让推理更简洁
zh.desc: OPSDC（政策自蒸馏推理压缩）是一种通过让模型从自身输出中提炼简洁行为来实现高效推理模型压缩的方法。该方法通过给模型一个“简洁表达”的指令，生成教师日志，并最小化学生自身输出的每个token的反向KL散度。OPSDC能够在保持准确性的同时，显著减少token数量，尤其在处理简单问题时表现出色。实验结果显示，在Qwen3-8B和Qwen3-14B模型上，MATH-500任务的token减少率达到57-59%，同时准确率提高了9-16个百分点。

papers.title: Timer-S1: A Billion-Scale Time Series Foundation Model with Serial Scaling
papers.abstract: Abstract Timer-S1 is a scalable Mixture-of-Experts time series model with 8.3B parameters that uses serial scaling and novel TimeMoE blocks to improve long-term forecasting accuracy.  					AI-generated summary We introduce Timer-S1, a strong Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) time series foundation model with 8.3B total parameters, 0.75B activated parameters for each token, and a context length of 11.5K. To overcome the scalability bottleneck in existing pre-trained time series foundation models, we perform Serial Scaling in three dimensions: model architecture, dataset, and training pipeline. Timer-S1 integrates sparse TimeMoE blocks and generic TimeSTP blocks for Serial-Token Prediction (STP), a generic training objective that adheres to the serial nature of forecasting. The proposed paradigm introduces serial computations to improve long-term predictions while avoiding costly rolling-style inference and pronounced error accumulation in the standard next-token prediction. Pursuing a high-quality and unbiased training dataset, we curate TimeBench, a corpus with one trillion time points, and apply meticulous data augmentation to mitigate predictive bias. We further pioneer a post-training stage, including continued pre-training and long-context extension, to enhance short-term and long-context performance. Evaluated on the large-scale GIFT-Eval leaderboard, Timer-S1 achieves state-of-the-art forecasting performance, attaining the best MASE and CRPS scores as a pre-trained model. Timer-S1 will be released to facilitate further research.

ru.title: Масштабируемая модель экспертов для прогнозирования временных рядов нового поколения
ru.desc: Timer-S1 — это масштабируемая модель на основе архитектуры Mixture-of-Experts для прогнозирования временных рядов с 8.3 млрд параметров. Модель использует инновационные TimeMoE блоки и серийное масштабирование по трём измерениям: архитектуре, данным и процессу обучения. Авторы предложили новый подход Serial-Token Prediction, который улучшает долгосрочные прогнозы, избегая накопления ошибок при последовательных предсказаниях. Модель обучена на высококачественном датасете TimeBench с триллионом временных точек и достигает лучших результатов на бенчмарке GIFT-Eval.

en.title: Revolutionizing Time Series Forecasting with Timer-S1
en.desc: Timer-S1 is a powerful Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) model designed for time series forecasting, featuring 8.3 billion parameters. It employs Serial Scaling across model architecture, dataset, and training pipeline to enhance scalability and accuracy. The model introduces innovative TimeMoE blocks and a Serial-Token Prediction (STP) objective, which allows for improved long-term forecasting without the pitfalls of traditional methods. Additionally, Timer-S1 is trained on a vast dataset called TimeBench, which contains one trillion time points, ensuring high-quality predictions and minimizing bias.

zh.title: Timer-S1：提升时间序列预测的强大模型
zh.desc: Timer-S1是一种可扩展的混合专家时间序列模型，具有83亿个参数，旨在提高长期预测的准确性。该模型采用串行扩展技术，结合稀疏的TimeMoE模块和通用的TimeSTP模块，以适应时间序列预测的特点。为了克服现有模型的可扩展性瓶颈，Timer-S1在模型架构、数据集和训练流程三个维度上进行了优化。经过评估，Timer-S1在大型GIFT-Eval排行榜上取得了最先进的预测性能，成为预训练模型中的佼佼者。

papers.title: UltraDexGrasp: Learning Universal Dexterous Grasping for Bimanual Robots with Synthetic Data
papers.abstract: Abstract A bimanual robotic grasping framework is presented that generates diverse grasp data through optimization and planning, enabling effective zero-shot sim-to-real transfer with high success rates on novel objects.  					AI-generated summary Grasping is a fundamental capability for robots to interact with the physical world. Humans, equipped with two hands, autonomously select appropriate grasp strategies based on the shape, size, and weight of objects, enabling robust grasping and subsequent manipulation. In contrast, current robotic grasping remains limited, particularly in multi-strategy settings. Although substantial efforts have targeted parallel-gripper and single-hand grasping, dexterous grasping for bimanual robots remains underexplored, with data being a primary bottleneck. Achieving physically plausible and geometrically conforming grasps that can withstand external wrenches poses significant challenges. To address these issues, we introduce UltraDexGrasp, a framework for universal dexterous grasping with bimanual robots. The proposed data-generation pipeline integrates optimization-based grasp synthesis with planning-based demonstration generation, yielding high-quality and diverse trajectories across multiple grasp strategies. With this framework, we curate UltraDexGrasp-20M, a large-scale, multi-strategy grasp dataset comprising 20 million frames across 1,000 objects. Based on UltraDexGrasp-20M, we further develop a simple yet effective grasp policy that takes point clouds as input, aggregates scene features via unidirectional attention, and predicts control commands. Trained exclusively on synthetic data, the policy achieves robust zero-shot sim-to-real transfer and consistently succeeds on novel objects with varied shapes, sizes, and weights, attaining an average success rate of 81.2% in real-world universal dexterous grasping. To facilitate future research on grasping with bimanual robots, we open-source the data generation pipeline at https://github.com/InternRobotics/UltraDexGrasp.
affiliations: The Chinese University of Hong Kong

ru.title: Универсальный захват двумя руками: от симуляции к реальности без дополнительного обучения
ru.desc: Работа представляет UltraDexGrasp — фреймворк для универсального ловкого захвата двуручных роботов, который комбинирует оптимизационный синтез захватов с планированием демонстраций. Авторы создали большой датасет из 20 миллионов кадров разнообразных стратегий захвата на 1000 объектах, сгенерированный исключительно в симуляции. На основе этого датасета была обучена политика захвата с использованием архитектуры на базе облаков точек и механизма одностороннего внимания. Обученная только на синтетических данных модель достигает успешного нулевого переноса из симуляции в реальность с точностью 81.2% на новых объектах различных форм и размеров.

en.title: Empowering Bimanual Robots with Universal Dexterous Grasping
en.desc: This paper introduces UltraDexGrasp, a framework designed for bimanual robotic grasping that generates diverse grasp data through optimization and planning techniques. It addresses the limitations of current robotic grasping by creating a large-scale dataset, UltraDexGrasp-20M, which includes 20 million frames of grasping data across 1,000 different objects. The framework enables a grasp policy that utilizes point clouds and unidirectional attention to predict control commands, achieving effective zero-shot sim-to-real transfer. As a result, the policy demonstrates a high success rate of 81.2% when grasping novel objects in real-world scenarios.

zh.title: 双手机器人抓取的未来：UltraDexGrasp框架
zh.desc: 本文提出了一种双手机器人抓取框架，能够通过优化和规划生成多样化的抓取数据，从而实现高成功率的零-shot模拟到现实转移。该框架名为UltraDexGrasp，结合了基于优化的抓取合成和基于规划的演示生成，创建了一个包含2000万个帧的多策略抓取数据集。通过使用合成数据训练的抓取策略，机器人能够在面对形状、大小和重量各异的新物体时，保持高达81.2%的成功率。该研究为双手机器人抓取的未来研究提供了重要的数据生成工具和资源。

papers.title: Distribution-Conditioned Transport
papers.abstract: Abstract Distribution-conditioned transport framework enables generalization to unseen distribution pairs and supports semi-supervised learning for scientific applications.  					AI-generated summary Learning a transport model that maps a source distribution to a target distribution is a canonical problem in machine learning, but scientific applications increasingly require models that can generalize to source and target distributions unseen during training. We introduce distribution-conditioned transport (DCT), a framework that conditions transport maps on learned embeddings of source and target distributions, enabling generalization to unseen distribution pairs. DCT also allows semi-supervised learning for distributional forecasting problems: because it learns from arbitrary distribution pairs, it can leverage distributions observed at only one condition to improve transport prediction. DCT is agnostic to the underlying transport mechanism, supporting models ranging from flow matching to distributional divergence-based models (e.g. Wasserstein, MMD). We demonstrate the practical performance benefits of DCT on synthetic benchmarks and four applications in biology: batch effect transfer in single-cell genomics, perturbation prediction from mass cytometry data, learning clonal transcriptional dynamics in hematopoiesis, and modeling T-cell receptor sequence evolution.

ru.title: Транспорт между распределениями, который учится на невидимых данных
ru.desc: Статья представляет фреймворк распределительно-обусловленного транспорта (DCT), который позволяет моделям трансформации распределений обобщаться на невидимые пары распределений путём кондиционирования карт транспорта на выученные эмбеддинги исходного и целевого распределений. Фреймворк поддерживает полу-обучение, так как может обучаться на произвольных парах распределений и использовать данные из единственного условия для улучшения предсказания. DCT совместима с различными механизмами транспорта, включая потоковое согласование и модели на основе дивергенций (Вассерштейн, MMD). Эффективность подхода продемонстрирована на синтетических тестах и четырёх биологических приложениях: коррекция батч-эффектов в геномике одиночных клеток, предсказание возмущений и моделирование эволюции.

en.title: Generalizing Transport Models for Unseen Distributions
en.desc: The paper introduces a new framework called distribution-conditioned transport (DCT) that helps machine learning models generalize to new distribution pairs that were not seen during training. This is particularly useful in scientific applications where data can vary widely. DCT works by conditioning transport maps on learned embeddings of the source and target distributions, allowing for better predictions even with limited data. Additionally, it supports semi-supervised learning, meaning it can improve its predictions by using data from only one of the conditions.

zh.title: 分布条件传输：提升模型泛化能力的创新框架
zh.desc: 本文提出了一种分布条件传输框架（DCT），旨在解决机器学习中源分布与目标分布之间的映射问题。DCT能够处理在训练过程中未见过的分布对，从而实现更好的泛化能力。该框架支持半监督学习，可以利用仅在一个条件下观察到的分布来提高传输预测的准确性。我们在生物学的多个应用中展示了DCT的实际性能优势，包括单细胞基因组学中的批次效应转移等。
